summary: High‑energy 3/31‑87 Spectrum show shines with a blistering Jack Straw → Franklin’s run and tight setlist; the FM broadcast matrix captures the excitement though the mix is a bit muddy.
review: The March 31, 1987 Philadelphia concert is remembered for its scorching opening Jack Straw → Franklin’s, relentless Candyman, and an emotionally charged Brokedown Palace. Reviewers praise Jerry’s searing guitar work, tight rhythm section play, and a set that stays on fire from start to finish, while noting the FM broadcast matrix sounds good overall but can be muddied and bass‑heavy at times.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews overwhelmingly praise the performance (many 4–5 ★) with consistent standout song mentions; recording quality is described as decent but occasionally muddy, pulling the overall weighted score to just above four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: FM broadcast mix captures the band well – clear vocals and instrument separation are generally good, but several reviewers note a muddy mid‑range, bass rumble and occasional lack of instrument definition.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The show bursts out of the gate with an explosive Jack Straw → Franklin’s, maintains high energy through a tight first set, dips slightly in the second‑set pacing but rebounds with powerful jams (Estimated/Terrapin, U.J.B., Stella) and ends on an emotional Brokedown Palace.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for scorching solos, especially the Jack Straw lead‑in and multiple fiery leads in Franklin’s, Candyman and Stella.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as “bombing” with strong bass lines; a few mentions of his chime work on Ramble On Rose and solid support throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for roaring vocals (“keep us on the run!”) and enthusiastic delivery on Desolation Row, Candyman and Brokedown Palace.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (and occasionally Keith) receive brief nods for subtle background color on Desolation Row and overall tightness of the keyboards.
band_member_comments.Drums: Lively, active drumming highlighted in the first‑set “drums pounding” and second‑set drum segment described as high‑energy.
